output:
CVE-2023-7224 is a high-severity vulnerability affecting OpenVPN Connect versions 3.0 through 3.4.6 on macOS. This vulnerability allows local users to execute arbitrary code within external third-party libraries by exploiting the DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES environment variable.

The vulnerability is categorized as CWE-95, which pertains to improper code neutralization during dynamic evaluation. This type of vulnerability can lead to serious security risks, including remote code execution (RCE), especially if privilege escalation techniques are employed.

OpenVPN Connect running on macOS platforms within the specified versions is vulnerable. The MITRE ATT&CK technique associated with this vulnerability is T1574, focusing on process injection. Users are advised to implement mitigation strategies such as removing unnecessary permissions (M1040), applying application isolation (M1052), and performing code signing (M1051) to prevent exploitation.
